ground_truth.title: What is SEO (Search Engine Optimization)?
ground_truth.author: Camille Cunningham
ground_truth.main_content:
Do you have a website or are you thinking about creating one? And do you want to attract more people to your business? If the answer is yes, then there's no doubt about it: SEO should be part of your marketing efforts. It's a great way to build your brand and get people on your site. But what does it actually entail? In this post, we'll give you an understanding of what SEO is and how you can get started!

SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization. Alex Moss, Principal SEO at Yoast, defines it as 'both the art and science of improving a website, and pages within, to be as visible as possible for when people search for a relevant topic within any search platform.'

Search engines like Google use a process of crawling, indexing, and ranking. Crawlers collect pages and save them in an index. When someone searches, the search engine uses algorithms to determine relevant pages and their ranking.

SEO focuses on organic traffic from unpaid search results, unlike paid advertising. Key acronyms include SEM (all search marketing), SEA (search engine advertising), and PPC (pay-per-click model).

SEO is important because Google gets around 8.3 billion searches per day. It is intent-driven, aligning with existing demand rather than interrupting users. This makes it a powerful inbound marketing strategy for high-quality leads.

Types of SEO include Ecommerce SEO for online stores, Local SEO for geographic targeting, Video SEO for video content visibility, and News SEO for publishers.

The three pillars of SEO are Technical SEO (site performance, crawlability, security, structured data), On-page SEO (content quality, keywords, E-E-A-T, internal linking), and Off-page SEO (external link building, brand building, social media).

Ranking factors include content quality, relevance, usability, external and internal links, technical aspects, user experience, and brand presence.

SEO has evolved from simple keyword density tactics to focusing on user experience and high-quality content. In 2025, SEO focuses less on raw keywords and more on search intent across diverse platforms including social media and LLMs, with increased importance of video content.

AI tools like Google AI Overviews are changing how information is presented in search results, sometimes providing complete answers without requiring clicks. This shifts focus from just clicks to building a strong brand and being visible on different platforms.
with: SEO is both the art and science of improving a website, and pages within, to be as visible as possible for when people search for a relevant topic within any search platform.
The three pillars of SEO are Technical SEO, On-page SEO, and Off-page SEO, each critical for overall success.
Google, the most-used search engine got around 8.3 billion searches per day in 2024.
Showing up in their search results aligns with an existing demand—your customers are actively seeking information, products, or solutions.
In 2025, SEO will focus less on raw keywords and more so around search intent across diverse platforms like social media and LLMs.
